verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (hK2) is canonically a secreted serine protease expressed primarily in prostate luminal epithelium and secreted into prostatic fluid and seminal plasma—it has no TM domain and was historically not recognized as a surface target. However, a 2025 Clinical Cancer Research study (Shen et al.) systematically confirmed KLK2 cell-surface expression in prostate cancer cell lines and patient-derived tumors by FACS and multiplex immunofluorescence, establishing it as a surface target in prostate cancer (oncogenic/cell-state-restricted). Multiple therapeutics exploiting this surface pool are in clinical trials: a KLK2×CD3 bispecific T-cell engager (pasritamig, NCT04898634), 225Ac-labeled radioligand (NCT04644770), and CAR-T cells. Surface display is cancer-cell-restricted; normal prostate tissue secretes the protein rather than displaying it stably on the surface. This fits cell_state_induced (oncogenic transformation enabling surface expression of a canonically secreted protein).
